verses.text: 'Praise ye the LORD... for he is good,' yet who can declare all His mighty acts? Blessed are they who keep judgment. Praise opens the psalm of confession. Even as we own our sin, we begin by blessing the good God.

'Praise ye the LORD... for he is good,' yet who can declare all His mighty acts? Blessed are they who keep judgment. Praise opens the psalm of confession. Even as we own our sin, we begin by blessing the good God.

'Praise ye the LORD... for he is good,' yet who can declare all His mighty acts? Blessed are they who keep judgment. Praise opens the psalm of confession. Even as we own our sin, we begin by blessing the good God.
The psalmist asks to be remembered with God's favour to His people, to share their good and joy. He longs to belong to the saved. The believer's hope is to be counted among God's own.
The psalmist asks to be remembered with God's favour to His people, to share their good and joy. He longs to belong to the saved. The believer's hope is to be counted among God's own.
'We have sinned with our fathers,' who in Egypt did not grasp God's wonders and rebelled at the sea. He confesses inherited and personal sin. True repentance owns the long history of disobedience.
'We have sinned with our fathers,' who in Egypt did not grasp God's wonders and rebelled at the sea. He confesses inherited and personal sin. True repentance owns the long history of disobedience.

Nevertheless God saved them for His name's sake, rebuked the sea, and led them through; then 'believed they his words' and sang His praise. Mercy triumphed over their unbelief. God's deliverance, not their faith, was the wonder.

Nevertheless God saved them for His name's sake, rebuked the sea, and led them through; then 'believed they his words' and sang His praise. Mercy triumphed over their unbelief. God's deliverance, not their faith, was the wonder.

Nevertheless God saved them for His name's sake, rebuked the sea, and led them through; then 'believed they his words' and sang His praise. Mercy triumphed over their unbelief. God's deliverance, not their faith, was the wonder.

Nevertheless God saved them for His name's sake, rebuked the sea, and led them through; then 'believed they his words' and sang His praise. Mercy triumphed over their unbelief. God's deliverance, not their faith, was the wonder.

But they soon forgot His works, lusted in the desert, and 'he gave them their request; but sent leanness into their soul.' Granted cravings brought ruin. To get our way against God's will is its own punishment.

But they soon forgot His works, lusted in the desert, and 'he gave them their request; but sent leanness into their soul.' Granted cravings brought ruin. To get our way against God's will is its own punishment.

But they soon forgot His works, lusted in the desert, and 'he gave them their request; but sent leanness into their soul.' Granted cravings brought ruin. To get our way against God's will is its own punishment.
They envied Moses and Aaron, and the earth opened to swallow the rebels, fire consuming their company. Rebellion met swift judgment. To rise against God's appointed leaders is to rise against God.
They envied Moses and Aaron, and the earth opened to swallow the rebels, fire consuming their company. Rebellion met swift judgment. To rise against God's appointed leaders is to rise against God.
They envied Moses and Aaron, and the earth opened to swallow the rebels, fire consuming their company. Rebellion met swift judgment. To rise against God's appointed leaders is to rise against God.

They made a calf at Horeb, exchanging God's glory for an image, and He would have destroyed them but for Moses standing 'in the breach.' Intercession turned away wrath. One faithful pleader can stay the judgment a people deserve.

They made a calf at Horeb, exchanging God's glory for an image, and He would have destroyed them but for Moses standing 'in the breach.' Intercession turned away wrath. One faithful pleader can stay the judgment a people deserve.

They made a calf at Horeb, exchanging God's glory for an image, and He would have destroyed them but for Moses standing 'in the breach.' Intercession turned away wrath. One faithful pleader can stay the judgment a people deserve.

They despised the pleasant land and would not believe God's word, so He swore to scatter them among the nations. Unbelief forfeited the promise. Doubting God's word kept a generation out of their inheritance.

They despised the pleasant land and would not believe God's word, so He swore to scatter them among the nations. Unbelief forfeited the promise. Doubting God's word kept a generation out of their inheritance.

They despised the pleasant land and would not believe God's word, so He swore to scatter them among the nations. Unbelief forfeited the promise. Doubting God's word kept a generation out of their inheritance.

They despised the pleasant land and would not believe God's word, so He swore to scatter them among the nations. Unbelief forfeited the promise. Doubting God's word kept a generation out of their inheritance.

They joined themselves to Baal-peor, until Phinehas stood up and executed judgment, and it was 'counted... for righteousness.' Zeal for God stayed the plague. One man's faithfulness was honoured for all generations.

They joined themselves to Baal-peor, until Phinehas stood up and executed judgment, and it was 'counted... for righteousness.' Zeal for God stayed the plague. One man's faithfulness was honoured for all generations.

They joined themselves to Baal-peor, until Phinehas stood up and executed judgment, and it was 'counted... for righteousness.' Zeal for God stayed the plague. One man's faithfulness was honoured for all generations.

They joined themselves to Baal-peor, until Phinehas stood up and executed judgment, and it was 'counted... for righteousness.' Zeal for God stayed the plague. One man's faithfulness was honoured for all generations.
At the waters of strife they angered God, and even Moses 'spake unadvisedly with his lips.' Their provocation cost their leader dearly. The sin of a people can draw the best of men into rash failure.
At the waters of strife they angered God, and even Moses 'spake unadvisedly with his lips.' Their provocation cost their leader dearly. The sin of a people can draw the best of men into rash failure.
In the land they failed to drive out the nations, mingled with them, and learned their works. Disobedience led to compromise. Tolerated evil soon becomes adopted evil.
In the land they failed to drive out the nations, mingled with them, and learned their works. Disobedience led to compromise. Tolerated evil soon becomes adopted evil.
In the land they failed to drive out the nations, mingled with them, and learned their works. Disobedience led to compromise. Tolerated evil soon becomes adopted evil.
They sacrificed their sons and daughters to devils, defiling the land with blood. Idolatry sank to horror. A people who forsake God descend to cruelties that natural feeling forbids.
They sacrificed their sons and daughters to devils, defiling the land with blood. Idolatry sank to horror. A people who forsake God descend to cruelties that natural feeling forbids.
They sacrificed their sons and daughters to devils, defiling the land with blood. Idolatry sank to horror. A people who forsake God descend to cruelties that natural feeling forbids.
So God's wrath kindled and He gave them to their enemies, who oppressed them; many times He delivered, but they rebelled again. The sad cycle repeated. Their sin returned as surely as His mercy did.

So God's wrath kindled and He gave them to their enemies, who oppressed them; many times He delivered, but they rebelled again. The sad cycle repeated. Their sin returned as surely as His mercy did.

Yet He regarded their affliction when He heard their cry, remembered His covenant, and made even their captors pity them. Mercy outlasted their failures. God's covenant love kept finding them in their misery.

Yet He regarded their affliction when He heard their cry, remembered His covenant, and made even their captors pity them. Mercy outlasted their failures. God's covenant love kept finding them in their misery.

Yet He regarded their affliction when He heard their cry, remembered His covenant, and made even their captors pity them. Mercy outlasted their failures. God's covenant love kept finding them in their misery.

'Save us, O LORD our God,' they pray, gathering them from the nations to give thanks; 'blessed be the LORD God of Israel for ever.' Confession ends in praise and hope. Even a history of sin closes with trust in unfailing mercy.
